title: Document Requirement Rules — Configurable Per Life Event and Dependent Relationship
description: The system shall determine required documentation based on the life event type and dependent relationship. Document requirements shall be configurable (stored in seed data, not hardcoded). Each rule specifies: life event type, dependent relationship type, document type required, and whether it is mandatory or conditional.
configurationNote: Document requirement rules are stored in seed data. The system reads requirements at runtime — no hardcoded document logic in application code.

title: Life Event Document Upload Step in Employee Portal
description: The Life Event enrollment workflow in the Employee Portal shall include a dedicated document upload step. The upload step appears after the employee adds the dependent and before the review/submit step. The system identifies the required document type from the document requirement rules and presents a targeted upload prompt.
workflowPosition: Report Life Event → Select Event Type → Enter Event Date → Add Dependent → Upload Documentation → Review → Submit

uploadScreenElements.uploadControl: File chooser button with drag-and-drop support

title: File Validation — MIME Type, Size, Integrity, and Duplicate Detection
description: The system shall validate uploaded files before storing them. Validation occurs server-side using MIME type inspection (magic bytes), not client-provided MIME type. The system rejects files that fail any validation check and returns a specific error message.

validationChecks.note: SHA-256 hash matches an existing document for same employee + life event + document type

title: Document Metadata Schema — Full Lifecycle Tracking Per Document
description: Each uploaded document shall have a metadata record capturing its full lifecycle from upload through verification decision. The metadata record is immutable once created; state changes append new audit events.

metadataFields.uploadedBy: Actor (employee self-upload or HR Admin on-behalf)

title: Document Verification Queue — HR Admin and Benefits Admin Review Interface
description: HR Administrators and Benefits Administrators shall have access to a Document Verification Queue listing all documents in PENDING_REVIEW status. The queue is filterable and sortable. Each row provides a one-click action to open the document review screen.
queueBadge: Pending Review count displayed in navigation badge

title: Document Review Screen — Inline Preview with Verification Checklist
description: The document review screen shall display: employee identity, life event details, the uploaded document inline (PDF viewer or image), the AI extraction result if available, and a verification checklist. The reviewer completes the checklist before approving or rejecting.
reviewScreenSections: Employee info (name, ID, life event type, event date)
AI Verification Assistant panel (if REQ-DOC-013 is enabled)
marriageCertificateChecklist: Event date is consistent with reported life event date

title: Document Approval — Triggers Coverage Activation Workflow
description: When a reviewer approves a document, the system shall update the document status to APPROVED, mark the life event as VERIFIED, and trigger the coverage activation workflow. All downstream steps (eligibility recalculation, enrollment update, carrier transaction, payroll update, notification) are initiated automatically after human approval.

title: Document Rejection — Coded Reason Required, Employee Notified with Re-upload Option
description: When a reviewer rejects a document, a rejection reason code must be selected (and an optional comment added). The document status becomes REJECTED, and the employee receives a notification explaining why the document was rejected and providing a [Upload New Document] call-to-action. The life event remains in PENDING_DOCS status.

employeeNotificationContent.subject: Action Required: Document Verification Failed
employeeNotificationContent.body: Your {documentType} could not be verified. Reason: {rejectionReason}. Please upload a new document to continue your life event enrollment.

title: Coverage Hold — Dependent Coverage Not Activated Until Documentation is Approved
description: If a life event or dependent addition requires documentation that has not yet been approved, the affected dependent's coverage shall be placed in PENDING status. The carrier shall NOT be notified and payroll shall NOT be updated until documentation is approved. This prevents premature carrier enrollment of unverified dependents.

resolutionPaths.approved: Document approved → Coverage ACTIVATED → Carrier transmitted → Payroll updated
resolutionPaths.rejected: Document rejected → Employee re-uploads → Returns to PENDING_REVIEW

demoDisplay: Life Event: Marriage | Spouse: John White | Documentation: Pending Verification | Coverage: PENDING

title: Post-Approval Cascade — Automated Coverage Activation After Document Approval
description: Document approval triggers a fully automated cascade that activates coverage and updates all downstream systems. No additional manual action is required after the reviewer clicks [Approve]. The employee is notified when each stage completes.

cascadeSteps.action: Enrollment record updated — dependent coverage status → ACTIVE
Premium recalculated for updated coverage tier
Carrier transaction created — ADD dependent to carrier
Payroll deduction record updated for new premium

title: Employee Document Status Visibility — Life Event Progress Tracker
description: The Employee Portal shall display a life event progress tracker showing the employee the status of each stage in their life event and documentation workflow. The tracker provides visibility into where the process stands without the employee needing to contact HR.

title: Document Lifecycle Audit Trail — Every Event Recorded
description: The audit trail shall record every document lifecycle event: upload, view (by reviewer), AI extraction, approval, rejection, and coverage activation triggered by approval. Each audit event records: actor, role, timestamp, action, document ID, decision, and reason (if applicable).

title: AI Document Verification Assistant — Extract, Validate, and Recommend
description: The system shall include an AI Document Verification Assistant that processes uploaded documents to extract key fields, compares extracted values against the employee and dependent records, and produces a verification recommendation. The AI recommends APPROVE or FLAG FOR REVIEW — final decision is always made by a human reviewer.
validationChecks: Event date matches reported life event date
governancePrinciple: AI recommends; human reviewer makes final approval or rejection decision

title: Document Verification as a Reusable Platform Service
description: Document verification shall be implemented as a reusable platform service, not as logic specific to marriage events. The same service handles all dependent documentation types: marriage certificates, birth certificates, adoption documents, loss-of-coverage letters, and death certificates. The document requirement rules (REQ-DOC-001) drive which document type is required; the verification workflow is identical across all types.
serviceCapabilities: Determine required document type from life event + dependent relationship rules
Trigger downstream coverage activation cascade on approval
architecturalPrinciple: One service, configurable rules. Adding a new life event type requires only a new row in the document requirement rules catalog — no code changes.
